title: Nauclea pedicellata

The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. It contains over 13,000 species of plants, many of which are used for medicinal purposes. parts: Nauclea pedicellata, commonly known as the yellow mangosteen, is a plant native to tropical Africa. The medicinal parts of Nauclea pedicellata are primarily the root bark and the leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The root bark is known to have anthelmintic properties and is used to treat worm infestations, while the leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. preparations: Nauclea pedicellata, also known as Akuamma, is used in traditional medicine. The seeds of the plant are extracted to create herbal preparations, often used for pain relief and as an anti-inflammatory agent. Akuamma seeds contain a compound called akuammicine, which is believed to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations made from Akuamma seeds are traditionally used to treat a variety of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. side_effects: Nauclea pedicellata, commonly known as the Aningre tree, has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. However, its overuse can cause some side effects. The excessive consumption of Nauclea pedicellata can lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, it may also cause liver damage and kidney problems if taken in large doses for a prolonged period.
